questions.question: The `cp invocation` --no-preserve= option Do not preserve the specified attributes.
questions.explanation: The `cp invocation` --no-preserve= option Do not preserve the specified attributes.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` --no-preserve= option Form the name of each destination file by appending to the target directory a slash and the specified name of the source file.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` --reflink[= option Remove each existing destination file before attempting to open it (contrast with -f above).

questions.question: The `cp invocation` --remove-destination option Remove each existing destination file before attempting to open it (contrast with -f above).

questions.question: The `cp invocation` --remove-destination option A sparse file contains holes – a sequence of zero bytes that does not occupy any file system blocks; the ‘ read ’ system call reads these as zeros.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -T option Do not copy a non-directory that has an existing destination with the same or newer modification timestamp; instead, silently skip the file without failing.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -u option Do not copy a non-directory that has an existing destination with the same or newer modification timestamp; instead, silently skip the file without failing.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -u option This is similar to ‘ none ’, in that no files in the destination are replaced, but any skipped files are diagnosed and induce a failure.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -fail option This is similar to ‘ none ’, in that no files in the destination are replaced, but any skipped files are diagnosed and induce a failure.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -fail option Print the name of each file before copying it.

questions.question: The `cp invocation` -v option Print the name of each file before copying it.
